actionable_feedback: Add an explicit Verification (or Calculation) sheet in the workbook with plain, visible cells (no hidden cells) that programmatically compute and display: inputs, current open beds per hospital, accepted_by_hospital cells, total_accepted, overflow (= requested_surge - total_accepted), final occupancy percentages, final staff ratios, and boolean occupancy/staffing flags. Ensure these are simple numeric cells with visible formulas so automated checkers can parse them.

Include an in-workbook cell that demonstrates attempted allocation summing to 70 and an explicit overflow routing field (e.g., columns: A_alloc, B_alloc, C_alloc, ExternalOverflow) so a verifier can see how 70 would be allocated and how overflow is recorded.

Add a Sensitivity sheet covering at minimum +/-10% surge scenarios (e.g., 63, 70, 77 patients) with the same computed outputs for each scenario, and at least one staffing-variation scenario (e.g., nurses +/-10% or alternative staff_ratio assumptions) to show robustness of decisions.
Expose the nurse headcount input (do not rely purely on implied proxies) or provide an explicit override/input field and document the proxy formula clearly (occupied / staff_ratio).

Provide an auxiliary machine-readable artifact (CSV or small JSON) that summarizes the key Verification sheet outputs (accepted_by_hospitals, overflow, flags) so automated graders can parse numbers even if library limitations exist in the grader environment.

Update the memo to reference the new Verification sheet and sensitivity scenarios, include a short table showing a hypothetical 70-patient allocation with overflow, and document any assumptions and formula references (sheet and cell addresses) to aid reproducibility.

Optional but recommended: add a small 'Calculation trace' area showing the exact formulas used (text or formula-exported values) and a one-click export (or included CSV) of the Calculation sheet for deterministic verification.
artifact_specific_comments: public_health_memo.md: Clear and thorough; documents infeasibility and shows arithmetic that supports the recommended allocation. Please update to reference added workbook verification cells and sensitivity outputs.

bed_surge_plan.xlsx: Contains required tabs but does not expose the explicit 70-sum allocation + overflow cell or a formal +/-10% sensitivity table in a machine-readable verification area; formulas could not be parsed in the grading environment-add a CSV export or plain numeric Verification sheet.
feedback_summary: Required artifacts present and memo documents input data, arithmetic, feasibility conclusion (67 in-hospital beds, overflow=3), and a recommended feasible allocation. Score (0.85) reflects sound reasoning in the memo but deducts for (a) not exposing an in-spreadsheet allocation that explicitly handles the full 70 patients (i.e., an explicit attempted 70-sum allocation + overflow routing cell), and (b) incomplete formal sensitivity analysis per rubric (no explicit +/-10% surge scenario and no formal staffing-variation scenario in the workbook). Automated formula-level verification was blocked by the environment's inability to parse the XLSX; the memo supports manual reproduction but the workbook should include machine-readable verification outputs.

metadata_json.notes: Primary risk to acceptance is lack of explicit in-spreadsheet 70-sum allocation + overflow and limited machine-readable verification outputs. The memo is strong; small workbook additions should resolve remaining rubric concerns.
